item.title: React Hooks：管理状态和处理副作用的综合指南 2023 | React Hooks: A Comprehensive Guide to Managing State and Handling Side Effects 2023
item.description:
这意味着您可以管理状态、进行 API 调用以及基于类的组件可以通过功能组件执行的其他操作。

这不是一个详尽的列表，但这些是您 99% 的时间都会使用的钩子。

使用 useState 挂钩管理状态

相当于类组件中的this.state = {} 。

要使用 useState 挂钩，请在组件的顶层调用它：

 const [state, setState] = useState(initialState);

它返回一个恰好包含两个值的数组：当前状态和用于更新该状态的函数。

让我们仔细看看它们。

当你的组件第一次渲染时，状态等于initialState 。

它可以是任何有效的 JavaScript 值。

注意： setState函数仅更新下一次渲染的状态变量。 如果您在调用setState函数后读取状态变量，您仍然会获得调用之前屏幕上的旧值。

此外，如果您提供的值与当前状态相同，React 将跳过组件的重新渲染。

用法

要使用 useState 挂钩，您可以在组件的顶层调用它。

 import {useState} from “react” function (){ const [state, setState] = useState(''); }

更新文本字段

在这个简单的示例中， firstName和lastName状态变量保存一个字符串。 当您在输入字段中键入内容时， handleFirstName和handleLastName更新器函数会读取 DOM 中的输入并相应地更新 UI。

 import { useState } from 'react'; export default function MyInput() { const [firstName, setFirstName] = useState(''); const [lastName, setLastName] = useState(''); function handleFirstName(e) { setFirstName(e.target.value); } function handleLastName(e) { setLastName(e.target.value); } return ( <> <input value={firstName} onChange={handleFirstName} /> <input value={lastName} onChange={handleLastName} /> <p>{firstName} {lastName} is typing.</p> </> ); }

更新状态中的对象和数组

更新状态中的数组或对象时，不应直接改变状态。

这就是我的意思👇

 state.age = 42; // you should not directly mutate state.

相反，您应该用新状态替换旧状态。

 SetState({ ...old state, age: 42 })

这也适用于数组。

 state.push(“Banana”) // not recommended setState([...prevState, “Banana”])

状态中的对象示例

在此示例中，员工状态变量保存一个对象。 每个输入字段都有一个更改处理程序，该处理程序调用setEmployee函数来更新状态。 扩展语法(...)确保状态被替换并且不被改变。

 import { useState } from 'react'; export default function Employee() { const [employee, setEmployee] = useState({ firstName: “”, lastName: “”, job: '', }); return ( <> <label> First name: <input value={employee.firstName} onChange={e => { setEmployee({ ...employee, firstName: e.target.value }); }} /> </label> <label> Last name: <input value={employee.lastName} onChange={e => { setEmployee({ ...employee, lastName: e.target.value }); }} /> </label> <label> Job: <input value={employee.job} onChange={e => { setEmployee({ ...employee, job: e.target.value }); }} /> </label> <p> {employee.firstName}{' '} {employee.lastName}{' '} ({employee.job}) </p> </> ); }

状态数组示例

在此示例中，fruits 状态变量保存一个数组。 每个按钮处理程序调用setFruits函数来更新状态。 扩展语法 (...) 确保状态被替换并且不被改变。

 import { useState } from 'react'; import AddTodo from './AddTodo.js'; import TaskList from './TaskList.js'; let nextId = 3; const initialTodos = [ { id: 0, title: "'Buy milk', done: true }," { id: 1, title: "'Eat tacos', done: false }," { id: 2, title: "'Brew tea', done: false }," ]; export default function TaskApp() { const [todos, setTodos] = useState(initialTodos); function handleAddTodo(title) { setTodos([ ...todos, { id: nextId++, title: "title," done: false } ]); } function handleChangeTodo(nextTodo) { setTodos(todos.map(t => { if (t.id === nextTodo.id) { return nextTodo; } else { return t; } })); } function handleDeleteTodo(todoId) { setTodos( todos.filter(t => t.id !== todoId) ); } return ( <> <AddTodo onAddTodo={handleAddTodo} /> <TaskList todos={todos} onChangeTodo={handleChangeTodo} onDeleteTodo={handleDeleteTodo} /> </> ); }

使用useEffect钩子处理副作用

副作用是指不受 React 控制的操作。 常见的副作用包括：

  • 调用第三方 API
  • 与浏览器的本地存储交互
  • 与 DOM 通信
  • 实现某些库，例如：动画库。

要使用useEffect挂钩，请在组件的顶层声明它

import React, { useEffect } from 'react'; function ExampleComponent() { useEffect(() => { // Side effect code goes here // Clean up the effect (optional) return () => { // Cleanup code goes here }; }, []); return ( // Component JSX goes here ); } export default ExampleComponent;

第一个参数是一个回调函数，其中包含副作用的代码。 您可以在此处执行 API 调用、操作 DOM、与浏览器存储交互或与外部库集成。

第二个参数是依赖项数组，它确定效果何时运行。 通过传递空数组[] ，效果将仅在初始渲染后运行一次。
如果您希望在某些依赖项更改时再次运行该效果，可以将它们包含在依赖项数组中。

现在您已经很好地了解了useEffect钩子及其在 React 中处理副作用的用途，让我们看一些常见的用例，看看如何在实践中使用它。

调用第三方API

在本例中，我们将了解如何使用 React 中的useEffect钩子来调用第三方 API。 作为前端开发人员，进行 API 调用是您经常做的事情，而useEffect钩子提供了一种处理此类异步操作的便捷方法。

我们将使用 IMDB API 来检索电影的详细信息。 让我们深入研究代码，看看它是如何完成的。

 import React, { useEffect, useState } from 'react'; function FilmDetails() { const [film, setFilm] = useState(null); useEffect(() => { // Function to fetch film details from the IMDB API const fetchFilmDetails = async () => { try { const response = await fetch('https://api.example.com/films/123'); const data = await response.json(); setFilm(data); } catch (error) { console.error('Error fetching film details:', error); } }; fetchFilmDetails(); // Cleanup function return () => { // Cleanup code goes here // This will be executed when the component is unmounted or when the dependency changes }; }, []); if (!film) { return <p>Loading film details...</p>; } return ( <div> <h2>{film.title}</h2> <p>{film.description}</p> {/* Render other film details */} </div> ); } export default FilmDetails;

在此示例中，我们有一个功能组件FilmDetails 。 我们还使用useState挂钩来管理最初设置为null film状态。
在useEffect挂钩内，我们定义一个异步函数来从 IMDB API 获取数据。

如果 API 调用成功，我们将使用setFilm使用检索到的数据更新film状态。

如果 API 调用期间发生错误，我们会将错误消息记录到catch块中的控制台。

我们传递了一个空的依赖数组作为第二个参数。 这意味着该效果只会在初始渲染时运行一次。

为了更好的用户体验，我们添加了加载状态，以防电影数据可用。 一旦可用，我们就渲染组件 JSX。

最后， useEffect中的return语句返回一个清理函数，该函数将在组件卸载或依赖项发生变化时执行。 您可以在清理函数中添加任何必要的清理代码，例如取消正在进行的请求或删除事件侦听器。

如果 API 调用成功，您应该会在浏览器中看到所获取的影片详细信息。

与浏览器的本地存储交互

在此示例中，我们将读取和写入浏览器的本地存储。
浏览器的本地存储用于持久数据。

让我们深入研究代码。

 import React, { useEffect, useState } from 'react'; function LocalStorageExample() { const [name, setName] = useState(''); useEffect(() => { // Read from local storage const storedName = localStorage.getItem('name'); if (storedName) { setName(storedName); } }, []); const handleNameChange = (e) => { const newName = e.target.value; setName(newName); // Write to local storage localStorage.setItem('name', newName); }; return ( <div> <h2>Hello, {name || 'stranger'}!</h2> <input type="text" value={name} onChange={handleNameChange} /> </div> ); } export default LocalStorageExample;

在此示例中，我们定义了一个功能组件LocalStorageExample 。 我们使用useState挂钩来管理name状态。

我们使用useEffect钩子与本地存储进行交互。 首先，我们使用getItem()方法从本地存储中读取数据。 如果name键存在，我们使用setName函数将name状态设置为值。
接下来，我们定义一个handleNameChange函数，当input的值更改时会触发该函数。 它使用新值更新名称状态，并使用localStorage.setItem()将新值写入本地存储，并将其与键“name”相关联。

该组件呈现一个标题，其中显示带有当前名称（如果未设置名称，则stranger ）的问候语，以及一个用户可以在其中输入新名称的输入字段。

注意：此代码在服务器端不起作用，因为 localStorage 仅存在于浏览器中。

使用 Hooks 将类组件重构为功能组件

在本节中，我将向您展示如何使用钩子将类组件重构为功能组件。

重构状态管理：从类组件到功能组件

为了将具有状态的类组件重构为具有状态的功能组件，我们使用this.state和this.setState将类组件的状态管理转换为利用 useState 钩子的功能组件。

类组件

import React, { Component } from 'react'; class ClassComponentWithState extends Component { constructor(props) { super(props); this.state = { count: 0, }; } incrementCount = () => { this.setState({ count: this.state.count + 1 }); }; render() { return ( <div> <p>Count: {this.state.count}</p> <button onClick={this.incrementCount}>Increment</button> </div> ); } } export default ClassComponentWithState;

对应功能组件

import React, { useState } from 'react'; function FunctionalComponentWithState() { const [count, setCount] = useState(0); const incrementCount = () => { setCount(count + 1); }; return ( <div> <p>Count: {count}</p> <button onClick={incrementCount}>Increment</button> </div> ); } export default FunctionalComponentWithState;

我们通过使用useState钩子替换this.state和this.setState将类组件转换为函数组件。 如上所述， useState挂钩返回一个包含两个值的数组：当前状态和更新后的函数。

重构数据获取：从类组件到功能组件

为了将从类组件获取数据重构为功能组件，我们将类组件的方法（通常使用诸如componentDidMount和componentDidUpdate之类的生命周期方法）转换为利用useEffect钩子来处理数据获取和更新组件状态的功能组件。

具有数据获取功能的类组件

import React, { Component } from 'react'; class ClassComponentWithDataFetching extends Component { constructor(props) { super(props); this.state = { data: [], updateCount: 0, }; } componentDidMount() { // Data fetching this.fetchData(); } componentDidUpdate(prevProps, prevState) { // Check if updateCount has changed if (prevState.updateCount !== this.state.updateCount) { this.fetchData(); } } fetchData = () => { // Data fetching fetch('https://api.example.com/data') .then((response) => response.json()) .then((jsonData) => this.setState({ data: jsonData })) .catch((error) => console.error('Error fetching data:', error)); }; handleUpdate = () => { this.setState((prevState) => ({ updateCount: prevState.updateCount + 1, })); }; render() { return ( <div> {this.state.data.map((item) => ( <p key={item.id}>{item.name}</p> ))} <button onClick={this.handleUpdate}>Update</button> </div> ); } } export default ClassComponentWithDataFetching

具有数据获取功能的组件

import React, { useEffect, useState } from 'react'; function FunctionalComponentWithDataFetching() { const [data, setData] = useState([]); const [updateCount, setUpdateCount] = useState(0); useEffect(() => { // Data fetching const fetchData = async () => { try { const response = await fetch('https://api.example.com/data'); const jsonData = await response.json(); setData(jsonData); } catch (error) { console.error('Error fetching data:', error); } }; fetchData(); }, [updateCount]); // Update when updateCount changes // Simulating component update triggering the useEffect const handleUpdate = () => { setUpdateCount((prevCount) => prevCount + 1); }; return ( <div> {data.map((item) => ( <p key={item.id}>{item.name}</p> ))} <button onClick={handleUpdate}>Update</button> </div> ); } export default FunctionalComponentWithDataFetching;

我们通过使用useEffect钩子替换componentDidMount和componentDidUpdate来转换类组件。 依赖数组帮助我们模仿这两种生命周期方法。

使用 React hooks 的好处

使用 React hooks 为开发 React 应用程序提供了几个好处：

  1. 简单
    钩子使功能组件能够拥有自己的状态和生命周期方法。 这使得更容易阅读、编写和理解代码，而无需处理类组件的复杂性。

  2. 可重复使用性
    自定义挂钩允许您将逻辑和有状态行为封装到可在应用程序的多个组件中使用的可重用函数中。 这促进了模块化和可维护的代码。

  3. 增强功能组件
    挂钩使功能组件能够具有状态和副作用，而以前仅在类组件中可用。 您可以使用useState钩子管理组件状态，并使用useEffect钩子处理副作用，使功能组件更加强大和通用。

  4. 提高性能
    使用useMemo和useCallback钩子，您可以分别记住值和函数，从而防止不必要的计算或组件的重新渲染。

  5. 更容易测试
    钩子促进了函数式编程概念的发展，这使得测试变得更加容易，因为逻辑被分成了小的、可测试的函数。 这允许您单独测试组件。

  6. 从类组件迁移
    挂钩简化了从类组件迁移到功能组件的过程。 现有的类组件可以使用钩子重构为功能组件，保留功能的同时提高代码的可读性和可维护性。

  7. 社区支持
    这意味着您可以找到充足的资源、文档和社区驱动的库来帮助有效学习和使用钩子。

结论性思考

它们支持状态管理、处理副作用以及将类组件重构为功能组件。

挂钩具有代码简单、可重用性以及消除对类组件的需求等优点。

总的来说，React hooks 提供了一种高效且有效的方法来增强 React 应用程序的功能和开发体验。

在Twitter和LinkedIn上关注我以获取更多内容和更新。

米凯洛·科卡迪 - 6 月 16 日

一旦暂停，chideracode 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到其暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，chideracode 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，chideracode 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己才能访问。

如果 chideracode 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从仪表板重新发布帖子。

笔记：

感谢您保证 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以执行以下操作来标记 chideracode：

使 chideracode 的所有帖子不那么明显

举报其他不当行为

取消 chideracode 的标记将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

React hooks are functions that let you use different React features from your functional components. This means that you can manage state, make API calls, and other things a class-based component can do from functional components.

There are different hooks in React which let you do different things.

  • useState hook lets you create and manage state.
  • useEffect hook lets you handle side-effects in your React application
  • useContext hook lets you share state without prop drilling, awesome right?
  • useMemo hook: lets you memoize your react components
  • useCallback hook is used to memoize functions, optimizing performance by preventing unnecessary re-rendering of components that depend on those functions.
  • useReducer hook: this hook is similar to the useState hook but handles complex state more efficiently.
  • useRef hook provides a way to create mutable references to elements or values that persist across renders.

This is not an exhaustive list but these are the hooks you will use 99% of the time.

Managing state with useState hook

The useState hook lets you add and manage state in a React component. It’s equivalent to this.state = {} in class components.

To use the useState hook, call it at the top-level of your component:

const [state, setState] = useState(initialState);

It returns an array of exactly two values: current state and a function for updating that state.

Let’s look at them closely.

state: this is the current state. When your component first renders, the state equals the initialState.

setState: this is a function for updating the state and trigger a re-render.

initialState: the value you want the state to be initially. It can be of any valid JavaScript value.

Note: The setState function only updates the state variable for the next render. If you read the state variable after calling the setState function, you will still get the old value that was on the screen before your call.

Also, React will skip re-rendering of your component if the value you provide is identical to the current state.

Usage

To use the useState hook, you call it at the top level of your component.

import {useState} from “react” function (){
const [state, setState] = useState(‘’);
}

Basic applications of useState

Updating a text field

In this simple example, the firstName and lastName state variables hold a string. When you type into the input fields, the handleFirstName and handleLastName updater functions read your inputs in the DOM and updates the UI accordingly.

import { useState } from 'react'; export default function MyInput() { const [firstName, setFirstName] = useState(‘’); const [lastName, setLastName] = useState(‘’); function handleFirstName(e) { setFirstName(e.target.value); }
function handleLastName(e) { setLastName(e.target.value); } return ( <> <input value={firstName} onChange={handleFirstName} /> <input value={lastName} onChange={handleLastName} /> <p>{firstName} {lastName} is typing.</p> </> );
}

Updating objects and arrays in state

When updating arrays or objects in state, you should not directly mutate the state.

Here’s what I mean 👇

state.age = 42; // you should not directly mutate state.

Instead, you should replace the old state with a new one.

SetState({
...old state, age: 42
})

This is also applicable to arrays.

state.push(“Banana”) // not recommended  setState([...prevState, “Banana”])

Example of object in state

In this example, the employee state variable holds an object. Each input field has a change handler that calls the setEmployee function to update the state. The spread syntax (...) to make sure state is replaced and not mutated.

import { useState } from 'react'; export default function Employee() { const [employee, setEmployee] = useState({ firstName: “”, lastName: “”, job: '', }); return ( <> <label> First name: <input value={employee.firstName} onChange={e => { setEmployee({ ...employee, firstName: e.target.value }); }} /> </label> <label> Last name: <input value={employee.lastName} onChange={e => { setEmployee({ ...employee, lastName: e.target.value }); }} /> </label> <label> Job: <input value={employee.job} onChange={e => { setEmployee({ ...employee, job: e.target.value }); }} /> </label> <p> {employee.firstName}{' '} {employee.lastName}{' '} ({employee.job}) </p> </> );
}

Example of array in state

In this example, the fruits state variable holds an array. Each button handler that calls the setFruits function to update the state. The spread syntax (...) to make sure state is replaced and not mutated.

import { useState } from 'react';
import AddTodo from './AddTodo.js';
import TaskList from './TaskList.js'; let nextId = 3;
const initialTodos = [ { id: 0, title: "'Buy milk', done: true }," { id: 1, title: "'Eat tacos', done: false }," { id: 2, title: "'Brew tea', done: false },"
]; export default function TaskApp() { const [todos, setTodos] = useState(initialTodos); function handleAddTodo(title) { setTodos([ ...todos, { id: nextId++, title: "title," done: false } ]); } function handleChangeTodo(nextTodo) { setTodos(todos.map(t => { if (t.id === nextTodo.id) { return nextTodo; } else { return t; } })); } function handleDeleteTodo(todoId) { setTodos( todos.filter(t => t.id !== todoId) ); } return ( <> <AddTodo onAddTodo={handleAddTodo} /> <TaskList todos={todos} onChangeTodo={handleChangeTodo} onDeleteTodo={handleDeleteTodo} /> </> );
}

Handling side effects with useEffect hook

The useEffect hook is used to handle side effects in React. Side effects mean operations not controlled by React. Common side effects include:

  • Making calls to a third-party API
  • Interacting with the browser’s local storage
  • Communicating with the DOM
  • Implementing certain libraries such as: animation libraries.

To use the useEffect hook, declare it at the top-level of your component

import React, { useEffect } from 'react'; function ExampleComponent() { useEffect(() => { // Side effect code goes here // Clean up the effect (optional) return () => { // Cleanup code goes here }; }, []); return ( // Component JSX goes here );
} export default ExampleComponent;

The useEffect hook takes two arguments:

The first argument is a callback function that contains the code for the side effect. This is where you can perform API calls, manipulate the DOM, interact with browser storage, or integrate with external libraries.

The second argument is the dependency array, which determines when the effect should run. By passing an empty array [], the effect will only run once, after the initial render.
If you want the effect to run again when certain dependencies change, you can include them in the dependency array.

Now that you have a good understanding of the useEffect hook and its purpose in handling side effects in React, let's look at some common use cases and see how it can be used in practice.

Making calls to third-party APIs

In this example, we'll look at how to use the useEffect hook in React to make calls to third-party APIs. Making API calls is something you will do more often as a front-end developer, and the useEffect hook provides a convenient way to handle such asynchronous operations.

We are going to use the IMDB API to retrieve details of a film. Let's dive into the code to see how it's done.

import React, { useEffect, useState } from 'react'; function FilmDetails() { const [film, setFilm] = useState(null); useEffect(() => { // Function to fetch film details from the IMDB API const fetchFilmDetails = async () => { try { const response = await fetch('https://api.example.com/films/123'); const data = await response.json(); setFilm(data); } catch (error) { console.error('Error fetching film details:', error); } }; fetchFilmDetails(); // Cleanup function return () => { // Cleanup code goes here // This will be executed when the component is unmounted or when the dependency changes }; }, []); if (!film) { return <p>Loading film details...</p>; } return ( <div> <h2>{film.title}</h2> <p>{film.description}</p> {/* Render other film details */} </div> );
} export default FilmDetails;

In this example, we have a functional component, FilmDetails. We also use the useState hook to manage the film state which is initially set to null.
Inside the useEffect hook, we define an asynchronous function to fetch data from the IMDB API.

If the API call succeeds, we update the film state with the retrieved data using setFilm.

If an error occurs during the API call, we log the error message to the console within the catch block.

We passed an empty dependency array as the second argument. This means that the effect will run only once—on initial render.

For better user experience, we have added a loading state in case the film data is yet available. Once it is available, we render the component JSX.

Finally, the return statement inside the useEffect returns a clean-up function, which will be executed when the component is unmounted or when the dependency changes. You can add any necessary clean-up code, such as canceling ongoing requests or removing event listeners, inside the clean-up function.

If the API call was successful, you should see the fetched film details displayed in your browser.

Interacting with the browser's local storage

In this example, we are going to reading and writing to the browser's local storage.
The browser's local storage is used for persisting data.

Let's dive into the code.

import React, { useEffect, useState } from 'react'; function LocalStorageExample() { const [name, setName] = useState(''); useEffect(() => { // Read from local storage const storedName = localStorage.getItem('name'); if (storedName) { setName(storedName); } }, []); const handleNameChange = (e) => { const newName = e.target.value; setName(newName); // Write to local storage localStorage.setItem('name', newName); }; return ( <div> <h2>Hello, {name || 'stranger'}!</h2> <input type="text" value={name} onChange={handleNameChange} /> </div> );
} export default LocalStorageExample;

In this example, we define a functional component, LocalStorageExample. We use the useState hook to manage the name state.

We use the useEffect hook to interact with the local storage. First, we read from the local storage using the getItem() method. If name key exists, we set the name state to the value using the setName function.
Next, we define a handleNameChange function that is triggered when the input's value changes. It updates the name state with the new value and uses localStorage.setItem() to write the new value to the local storage, associating it with the key 'name'.

The component renders a heading that displays the greeting with the current name (or stranger if no name is set), and an input field where the user can enter a new name.

Note: This code will not work in server-side as the localStorage only exists in the browser.

Refactoring Class Components to Functional Components with Hooks

Hooks were only introduced in React 16. This means that if you have been using React before then, you will most likely be using class components for tracking and managing data, as well as fetching and sending data.

In this section, I am going to show you how to refactor your class components to functional components using hooks.

Refactoring State Management: From Class Components to Functional Components

To refactor class components with state to functional components with state, we convert the class component's state management using this.state and this.setState to functional components utilizing the useState hook.

Class component

import React, { Component } from 'react'; class ClassComponentWithState extends Component { constructor(props) { super(props); this.state = { count: 0, }; } incrementCount = () => { this.setState({ count: this.state.count + 1 }); }; render() { return ( <div> <p>Count: {this.state.count}</p> <button onClick={this.incrementCount}>Increment</button> </div> ); }
} export default ClassComponentWithState;

Corresponding functional component

import React, { useState } from 'react'; function FunctionalComponentWithState() { const [count, setCount] = useState(0); const incrementCount = () => { setCount(count + 1); }; return ( <div> <p>Count: {count}</p> <button onClick={incrementCount}>Increment</button> </div> );
} export default FunctionalComponentWithState;

We converted the class component to functional component by replacing this.state and this.setState with the useState hook. As explained above, the useState hook returns an array with exactly two values: current state and an updated function.

Refactoring Data Fetching: From Class Components to Functional Components

To refactor data fetching from class components to functional components, we convert the class component's approach, which typically uses lifecycle methods like componentDidMount and componentDidUpdate, to functional components utilizing the useEffect hook for handling data fetching and updating component state.

Class component with data fetching

import React, { Component } from 'react'; class ClassComponentWithDataFetching extends Component { constructor(props) { super(props); this.state = { data: [], updateCount: 0, }; } componentDidMount() { // Data fetching this.fetchData(); } componentDidUpdate(prevProps, prevState) { // Check if updateCount has changed if (prevState.updateCount !== this.state.updateCount) { this.fetchData(); } } fetchData = () => { // Data fetching fetch('https://api.example.com/data') .then((response) => response.json()) .then((jsonData) => this.setState({ data: jsonData })) .catch((error) => console.error('Error fetching data:', error)); }; handleUpdate = () => { this.setState((prevState) => ({ updateCount: prevState.updateCount + 1, })); }; render() { return ( <div> {this.state.data.map((item) => ( <p key={item.id}>{item.name}</p> ))} <button onClick={this.handleUpdate}>Update</button> </div> ); }
} export default ClassComponentWithDataFetching

Functional component with data fetching

import React, { useEffect, useState } from 'react'; function FunctionalComponentWithDataFetching() { const [data, setData] = useState([]); const [updateCount, setUpdateCount] = useState(0); useEffect(() => { // Data fetching const fetchData = async () => { try { const response = await fetch('https://api.example.com/data'); const jsonData = await response.json(); setData(jsonData); } catch (error) { console.error('Error fetching data:', error); } }; fetchData(); }, [updateCount]); // Update when updateCount changes // Simulating component update triggering the useEffect const handleUpdate = () => { setUpdateCount((prevCount) => prevCount + 1); }; return ( <div> {data.map((item) => ( <p key={item.id}>{item.name}</p> ))} <button onClick={handleUpdate}>Update</button> </div> );
} export default FunctionalComponentWithDataFetching;

We converted the class component by replacing the componentDidMount andcomponentDidUpdatewith the useEffect hook. The dependency array helps us mimick the two lifecycle methods.

Benefits of Using the React hooks

Using React hooks provides several benefits in developing React applications:

  1. Simplicity
    Hooks enable functional components to have their own state and lifecycle methods. This makes it easier to read, write, and understand code without dealing with the complexity of class components.

  2. Reusability
    Hooks promote code reuse. Custom hooks allow you to encapsulate logic and stateful behavior into reusable functions that you used across multiple components in your application. This promotes modular and maintainable code.

  3. Enhanced Functional Components
    Hooks enable functional components to have state and side effects, previously only available in class components. You can manage component state using the useState hook and handle side effects with the useEffect hook, making functional components more powerful and versatile.

  4. Improved Performance
    Hooks optimize performance by reducing unnecessary re-renders. With the useMemo and useCallback hooks, you can memoize values and functions respectively, preventing unnecessary computations or re-rendering of components.

  5. Easier Testing
    Hooks promote functional programming concepts which makes testing easier, as the logic is separated into small, testable functions. This allows you to test components in isolation.

  6. Migration from Class Components
    Hooks simplify the process of migrating from class components to functional components. Existing class components can be refactored to functional components using hooks, retaining the functionality while improving code readability and maintainability.

  7. Community Support
    Hooks have gained widespread adoption and support from the React community. This means that you can find ample resources, documentation, and community-driven libraries to assist in learning and using hooks effectively.

Concluding Thoughts

React hooks are a powerful feature that allows developers to utilize different React functionalities within functional components. They enable state management, handling side effects, and refactoring class components to functional components.

The useState hook is used for managing state, while the useEffect hook is used for handling side effects such as making API calls. Hooks provide benefits such as code simplicity, reusability, and eliminating the need for class components.

Overall, React hooks offer an efficient and effective way to enhance the functionality and development experience in React applications.

Follow me on Twitter and LinkedIn for more content and updates.

Mykhailo Kokadii - Jun 16

item.title: 使用 F-Log 简化 Node.js 中的文件日志记录 | Simplify File Logging in Node.js with F-Log
item.description:
迦勒·奥克帕拉

发表于 6 月 24 日

介绍：
日志记录是任何应用程序的一个重要方面，它允许开发人员跟踪事件、错误和重要信息。 为了简化 Node.js 中的文件日志记录，我创建了 F-Log，这是一个多功能的 npm 包。 借助 F-Log，您可以轻松地将强大的日志记录功能融入到您的项目中，使调试和故障排除变得轻而易举。

安装：要开始使用 F-Log，只需使用 npm 安装软件包：

用法：
安装后，您可以使用 F-Log 轻松创建日志。 以下是如何使用该包的一些示例：

  1. 创建单个日志：要创建单个日志，请使用log函数：
 import { log } from '@drantaz/f-log'; log('Hello World', 'info'); // This will print 'Hello World' to the console // in a blue color and save it to a file named // 'logs.log'

将其设置为false将阻止保存日志。

  1. 记录多个文本：如果需要一次记录多个文本，可以使用logAll函数：
 import { logAll } from '@drantaz/f-log'; logAll('info', true, 'Log 1', 'Log 2', 'Log 3', ...); // This will log each text and save them in // the 'logs.log' file

与log功能类似， logAll的第二个参数可以用来控制是否将日志持久化到文件中。

阅读日志：
要检索日志，您可以使用getLogs函数：

 import { getLogs } from '@drantaz/f-log'; // Get all logs without grouping const logs = getLogs(false); // Get all logs with grouping const logs = getLogs(); // Get logs by title without grouping const logs = getLogs(false, 'info'); // Get logs by title with grouping const logs = getLogs(true, 'info');

默认情况下，日志以数组形式返回。 您可以通过指定分组参数并按标题过滤日志来自定义输出。

配置：
要自定义 F-Log 的配置，您可以在项目的根目录下创建一个名为f-log.json的文件。 以下是如何覆盖日志文件路径的示例：

未来版本：
我正在积极致力于为 F-Log 添加更多配置选项和增强功能。 请继续关注未来的版本以解锁更多功能。

日志类型：
地位颜色信息蓝色的成功绿色的警告橙子错误红色的批判的深红

结论：
使用 F-Log，您可以简化 Node.js 应用程序中的文件日志记录。 通过轻松整合强大的日志记录功能，您可以简化调试并获得对应用程序行为的宝贵见解。 尝试一下 F-Log，让您的日志记录更上一层楼！

一旦被暂停，drantaz 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到其暂停被取消。

一旦解除暂停，drantaz 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，drantaz 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己才能访问。

如果 drantaz 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从仪表板重新发布帖子。

笔记：

感谢您保证 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以执行以下操作来标记 drantaz：

使 drantaz 的所有帖子不那么明显

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 drantaz 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Caleb Okpara

Posted on Jun 24

Introduction:
Logging is an essential aspect of any application, allowing developers to track events, errors, and important information. To simplify file logging in Node.js, I've created F-Log, a versatile npm package. With F-Log, you can effortlessly incorporate robust logging capabilities into your projects, making debugging and troubleshooting a breeze.

Installation: To get started with F-Log, simply install the package using npm:

Usage:
Once installed, you can use F-Log to create logs with ease. Here are a few examples of how you can use the package:

  1. Creating a Single Log: To create a single log, use the log function: 
import { log } from '@drantaz/f-log'; log('Hello World', 'info');
// This will print 'Hello World' to the console 
// in a blue color and save it to a file named 
// 'logs.log'

The third parameter of the log function is optional and can be used to specify whether to persist the log to the file. Setting it to false will prevent the log from being saved.

  1. Logging Multiple Texts: If you need to log multiple texts at once, you can use the logAll function: 
import { logAll } from '@drantaz/f-log'; logAll('info', true, 'Log 1', 'Log 2', 'Log 3', ...);
// This will log each text and save them in 
// the 'logs.log' file

Similar to the log function, the second parameter of logAll can be used to control whether the logs should be persisted to the file.

Reading Logs:
To retrieve your logs, you can use the getLogs function:

import { getLogs } from '@drantaz/f-log'; // Get all logs without grouping
const logs = getLogs(false); // Get all logs with grouping
const logs = getLogs(); // Get logs by title without grouping
const logs = getLogs(false, 'info'); // Get logs by title with grouping
const logs = getLogs(true, 'info');

By default, the logs are returned as an array. You can customize the output by specifying the grouping parameter and filtering logs by title.

Configuration:
To customize the configuration of F-Log, you can create a file called f-log.json at the root of your project. Here's an example of how you can override the log file path:

Future Releases:
I am actively working on adding more configuration options and enhancements to F-Log. Stay tuned for future releases to unlock even more functionality.

Log Types:
F-Log supports various log types, each represented by a specific color:

Status Color
info blue
success green
warn orange
error red
critical darkred

Conclusion:
With F-Log, you can simplify file logging in your Node.js applications. By easily incorporating robust logging capabilities, you can streamline debugging and gain valuable insights into the behavior of your application. Give F-Log a try and take your logging to the next level!

www.npmjs.com/package/@drantaz/f-log

Ram Maheshwari ♾️ - Apr 16

Once unpublished, all posts by drantaz will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging drantaz will restore default visibility to their posts.

item.title: Ronkonkoma 污水池公司 | Cesspool company Ronkonkoma
item.description:
排水堵塞问题是家庭和办公室中最严重的问题。 人们经常受到这个问题的困扰，这还可能带来高额的水管维修和更换费用。 在它变得难以单独解决之前，它必须在早期阶段得到解决。 这种麻烦需要高超的技术理解和多年的经验。在家里遇到此类麻烦的人们通常会请水管工和专业的社区技术人员来解决问题。
排水沟清洁

当您发现水逐渐转移或排水管堵塞时，您必须考虑致电水管工检查整个管道系统。 下水道水流缓慢的根本原因是下水道堵塞问题。 餐布、灰尘、污泥、油脂、油甚至树根也可能导致排水管堵塞。 油脂、油和餐布积聚在排水管的内壁上。 它会影响管道的完整性并可能限制水流的正常流动。 它可能会导致钢管或 PVC 管内部出现细纹或大裂纹。 管道公司的专家通过他们专注的排水沟清洁服务提供现场缓解和长期安心。

下水道维修

有时，下水道管道会在室外破裂，给业主带来麻烦。 它不仅超出正常时间损坏整个下水道设备，而且还污染周围的空气。 它对健康有害，因此恢复甚至完全更换是最终的答案。 在大多数情况下，需要进行挖掘来修复损坏的下水道管道。

根部和油脂、除油

管道技术人员提供革命性的排水沟清洁解决方案，解决油脂、油、泥土、碎片和根问题。 管道工将 Jet Rooters 命名为 Jet Rooters，可以有效地清除下水道管道中的树根和油、污泥、油脂。 排水沟清理的答案是一种暂时性的问题。 它可以清理管道路线，促进管道内的水自由流动到下水道系统。

管道技术人员还使用视频检查方法等现代技术。 它是最方便的排水管清洁工具，用于诊断整个管道系统并查明损坏的管道。 该技术还用于发现地下管道裂缝、渗漏及相关问题。

管道爆裂

管道专家提供了一种非开挖技术来更换下水道管道。 这是一种在不破坏美丽景观和车道的情况下更新整个下水道管道的技术。 有时，希望将全新的下水道系统与金属管道连接起来，以便完全安心。



原文:

Drain blockading problem is the most sizeable problem in houses and offices. People are frequently plagued by way of this problem which may additionally bring about high-priced plumbing repairing and replacements. It have to be addresses at its early levels earlier than it receives too tough to address unmarried handedly. It is the hassle which needs superior technical understanding and years of revel in. People who come upon such troubles in their houses regularly name plumbers and expert neighborhood technicians to clear up the problem.
Cesspool company Ronkonkoma
Drain Cleaning

Whenever you discover gradual transferring water or clogged drains, you must take into account calling your plumber to check out the whole plumbing system. The essential reason at the back of slow moving of water via drains is the drain clogging hassle. Clogging in drains may additionally occur due to meals cloth, dust, sludge, grease, oil or even roots. Grease, oil and meals cloth get accumulated at the inside walls of the drain pipes. It can deter the integrity of the pipeline and may restriction the normal go with the flow of water. It can cause hair line or large cracks inside the steel or PVC pipes. The specialists at plumbing corporations deliver on the spot relief and long time peace of thoughts thru their devoted drain cleansing services.

Sewer Repair

Sometimes a sewer pipe breaks in the outside inflicting trouble to the house owners. It not only damages the entire sewer machine beyond regular time however also pollutes the surrounding air. It is injurious to health and consequently a restore or even a complete replacement is the final answer. In most of the cases excavation is needed in an effort to repair a damaged sewer pipe.

Roots and Grease, Oil Removal

Plumbing technicians offer revolutionary drain cleansing answers that solves the grease, oil, mud, & debris and roots problems. Plumbers name Jet Rooters to effectively cut roots and oil, sludge, grease from your sewer pipeline. It is a kind of temporary problem to the answer of drain cleaning. It can clear the pipe route to foster the free moving of water inside the pipes to the sewer system.

Plumbing technicians additionally make use of modern day strategies like video inspection method. It is the handiest drain cleansing gadget this is used to diagnose the overall plumbing system and pinpoint harm pipes. This technique is also used to discover the underground pipe crack, leakage and related problems.

Pipe Bursting

Plumbing specialists offer a trenchless technique for changing your sewer pipes. It is a technique to update your complete sewer pipes with out ruining stunning landscapes and driveways. Sometimes a brand new sewer system in conjunction with metallic pipes wishes to be hooked up for complete peace of mind.
item.author: deviationexcel03

item.title: 布尔玛 VS 顺风 | Bulma Vs Tailwind
item.description:
爱尔兰杰夫11

发表于 6 月 24 日

虽然它们有一些相似之处，但也有明显的差异。 让我们在几个关键领域比较 Bulma 和 Tailwind：

  1. 设计和造型：Bulma 提供了开箱即用的干净、现代的设计，具有一致且具有视觉吸引力的美感。 它具有各种用于样式元素的预定义组件和实用程序类。 另一方面，Tailwind 采用实用程序优先的方法，提供了一组广泛的实用程序类，您可以组合这些实用程序类来创建自定义设计。 它提供了高度灵活且可定制的造型体验。

  2. 灵活性和定制化：Tailwind 以其高水平的定制化而闻名。 借助其实用程序类，您可以轻松修改和微调设计的各个方面。 它允许您创建独特且复杂的布局，而无需编写自定义 CSS。 它在设计和造型选择方面更加固执己见。

  3. 学习曲线：与 Tailwind 相比，Bulma 的学习曲线更简单。 它提供了一组即用型组件，使其易于上手。 您可以使用其网格系统和预定义的类快速创建响应式布局。 另一方面，Tailwind 由于其广泛的实用程序类列表而具有更陡峭的学习曲线。 可能需要一些时间来熟悉类名称及其功能。

  4. 大小和性能：Bulma 的文件大小相对较小，可以被认为是轻量级的。 其默认样式和组件集旨在高效且优化。 另一方面，由于其实用程序类方法，Tailwind 可能会导致更大的 CSS 文件。 但是，通过正确的配置和优化，您可以通过清除未使用的样式来最小化最终文件大小，从而获得良好的性能。

  5. 社区和生态系统：Bulma 和 Tailwind 都有活跃的社区并提供资源、文档和支持。 他们拥有充满活力的生态系统，提供第三方集成、插件和扩展。 然而，截至 2021 年 9 月我的了解截止，Tailwind 已经变得更受欢迎，并且总体上拥有更大的社区。

最终，Bulma 和 Tailwind 之间的选择取决于您的具体项目要求、个人偏好和开发方法。 如果您更喜欢预先设计且固执己见的框架，让您快速启动并运行，那么 Bulma 是一个不错的选择。 比较 Bulma 和 Tailwind 时还需要考虑以下几点：

  1. 集成和组件生态系统：Bulma 提供了一组预定义的组件，例如导航栏、表单、模式等，可以轻松集成到您的项目中。 它提供了一组可以很好地协同工作的有凝聚力的组件。 另一方面，Tailwind 更注重提供实用程序类而不是预构建组件。 它允许您使用提供的实用程序类从头开始构建自己的组件。

  2. 浏览器兼容性：Bulma 和 Tailwind 都设计为可以在现代浏览器中良好运行。 它们使用现代浏览器支持的 CSS 功能，但也包括旧浏览器的后备和解决方法。 但是，在不同的浏览器上测试您的项目以确保兼容性始终是一个好习惯。

  3. 文档和学习资源：Bulma 和 Tailwind 都提供全面的文档和学习资源来帮助您入门。 他们提供示例、代码片段和指南来帮助您有效地理解和使用他们的框架。 此外，围绕这两个框架的社区都很活跃，并且经常提供教程、博客文章和支持论坛。

  4. 项目规模和范围：在考虑项目的规模和范围时，评估 Bulma 和 Tailwind 之间的权衡非常重要。 个人偏好和团队共识：最终，个人偏好和开发团队的共识在 Bulma 和 Tailwind 之间的选择中发挥着重要作用。 如果您或您的团队成员以前有经验，或者更喜欢一种框架而不是另一种框架，那么它可能会影响您的决定。 选择框架时值得考虑团队的熟悉程度和专业知识。

总之，Bulma 和 Tailwind 都是功能强大的 CSS 框架，它们提供了构建 Web 界面的不同方法。 考虑您的项目要求、设计偏好、学习曲线和团队动态，以就哪种框架最适合您的需求做出明智的决定。

关于作者。 一旦被暂停，irishgeoff11 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到其暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，irishgeoff11 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，irishgeoff11 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己才能访问。

如果 irishgeoff11 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从仪表板重新发布帖子。

笔记：

感谢您保证 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以执行以下操作来标记 irishgeoff11：

使 irishgeoff11 的所有帖子不那么明显

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 irishgeoff11 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Irishgeoff11

Posted on Jun 24

Bulma and Tailwind are both popular CSS frameworks that can be used to build responsive and stylish web interfaces. While they share some similarities, they also have distinct differences. Let's compare Bulma and Tailwind in a few key areas:

  1. Design and Styling: Bulma provides a clean and modern design out of the box, with a consistent and visually appealing aesthetic. It has a wide range of predefined components and utility classes for styling elements. On the other hand, Tailwind takes a utility-first approach, offering an extensive set of utility classes that you can combine to create custom designs. It provides a highly flexible and customizable styling experience.

  2. Flexibility and Customization: Tailwind is known for its high level of customization. With its utility classes, you can easily modify and fine-tune every aspect of your design. It allows you to create unique and intricate layouts without having to write custom CSS. Bulma, while customizable to some extent, doesn't offer the same level of granular control as Tailwind. It's more opinionated in terms of design and styling choices.

  3. Learning Curve: Bulma has a simpler learning curve compared to Tailwind. It provides a set of ready-to-use components, making it easy to get started. You can quickly create responsive layouts by using its grid system and predefined classes. Tailwind, on the other hand, has a steeper learning curve due to its extensive list of utility classes. It may take some time to become familiar with the class names and their functionalities.

  4. Size and Performance: Bulma has a relatively small file size and can be considered lightweight. Its default styling and component set are designed to be efficient and optimized. Tailwind, on the other hand, can result in a larger CSS file due to its utility class approach. However, with proper configuration and optimization, you can minimize the final file size by purging unused styles, resulting in good performance.

  5. Community and Ecosystem: Both Bulma and Tailwind have active communities and offer resources, documentation, and support. They have vibrant ecosystems with third-party integrations, plugins, and extensions available. However, as of my knowledge cutoff in September 2021, Tailwind has gained more popularity and has a larger community overall.

Ultimately, the choice between Bulma and Tailwind depends on your specific project requirements, personal preference, and development approach. Bulma is a good fit if you prefer a pre-designed and opinionated framework that gets you up and running quickly. Tailwind suits developers who value flexibility, customization, and want full control over their styles.

Here are a few more points to consider when comparing Bulma and Tailwind:

  1. Integration and Component Ecosystem: Bulma provides a set of predefined components like navigation bars, forms, modals, and more, which can be easily integrated into your project. It offers a cohesive set of components that work well together. Tailwind, on the other hand, focuses more on providing utility classes rather than prebuilt components. It allows you to build your own components from scratch using the utility classes provided.

  2. Browser Compatibility: Both Bulma and Tailwind are designed to work well across modern browsers. They use CSS features that are supported by modern browsers, but they also include fallbacks and workarounds for older browsers. However, it's always a good practice to test your projects on different browsers to ensure compatibility.

  3. Documentation and Learning Resources: Bulma and Tailwind both offer comprehensive documentation and learning resources to help you get started. They provide examples, code snippets, and guides to assist you in understanding and using their frameworks effectively. Additionally, the communities surrounding both frameworks are active and often provide tutorials, blog posts, and support forums.

  4. Project Size and Scope: When considering the size and scope of your project, it's important to evaluate the trade-offs between Bulma and Tailwind. Bulma's predefined components and opinionated design may be a better fit for smaller projects that require quick development. Tailwind's flexibility and customization options make it well-suited for larger projects where you have specific design requirements or the need for extensive customization.

  5. Personal Preference and Team Consensus: Ultimately, personal preference and the consensus of your development team play a significant role in choosing between Bulma and Tailwind. If you or your team members have prior experience or are more comfortable with one framework over the other, it may influence your decision. It's worth considering the familiarity and expertise of your team when selecting a framework.

In summary, both Bulma and Tailwind are powerful CSS frameworks that offer different approaches to building web interfaces. Bulma provides a ready-to-use design with predefined components, while Tailwind focuses on utility classes and customization. Consider your project requirements, design preferences, learning curve, and team dynamics to make an informed decision on which framework is the best fit for your needs.

About the author. Geoffrey Callaghan is a programmer at fabform.io working on the form backend service team.

item.title: 从头开始对机器人进行分析。 第 1 部分 - 编写机器人 | Do analytics for the bot from scratch. Part 1 - writing a bot
item.description:
马克西姆·拉马诺夫

发表于 6 月 24 日

在本文中，我们将探讨将基本分析连接到机器人的重要性以及它可以带来哪些好处。

介绍

在当今的数字世界中，与客户的互动越来越多地通过在线平台进行，创建和维护有效的聊天机器人正在成为成功企业的一个重要方面。 然而，构建一个仅提供信息或回答基本问题的机器人已经不足以获得竞争优势。 如今，了解用户行为和需求、分析数据并根据这些信息做出明智的决策非常重要。

这就是将分析连接到机器人可以解决问题的地方。 分析工具和方法的集成使您能够收集、分析和解释有关用户与机器人交互的数据。 这使我们有机会获得有价值的见解，这将有助于优化机器人的性能，改善用户体验并根据数据做出明智的决策。

将分析连接到机器人是动态数字时代聊天机器人开发和改进不可或缺的一部分。 它允许您将机器人从简单的通信工具转变为强大的数据分析工具，帮助您的企业制定有效的战略并实现其目标。 让我们看看分析与机器人的连接为我们带来的所有优势和机遇。

今天我们将收到哪些数据

在这个例子中，我们将学习：

  • 查看“活跃”和“死亡”用户的数量；
  • 查看通过机器人发送的消息数量；
  • 查看消息本身；
  • 构建图表。

工具

  • 首先，我们来写一个机器人

    作为示例，让我们编写一个简单的 Python 3 机器人，它允许您交换匿名消息。

    工作原理

    向用户提供指向机器人的个人链接，其中包含需要向其发送消息的用户的标识符。 他可以将其发布到他的社交网络上。 任何想要向用户发送匿名消息的人都必须点击此链接。 之后，机器人将接收/start命令以及 Telegram 中用户 ID 形式的有效负载。

    在 Telegram 中创建一个机器人

    转到@BotFather并创建一个机器人：

    连接图书馆

    我们连接用于创建电报机器人的库：

    也适用于使用 MongoDB：

    编写代码

    创建一个main.py文件并编写任何机器人的开头：

     bot = Bot(token='BotFather TOKEN HERE') storage = MemoryStorage() # storage that allows you to remember the states of dialogs with users dp = Dispatcher(bot, storage=storage)

    让我们添加一个需要发送消息的状态：

     class Stage(StatesGroup): send_message_to = State()

    让我们为/start命令编写一个处理程序：

     @dp.message_handler(commands=['start'], state=['*', Stage.send_message_to]) async def start(message: types.Message, state: FSMContext): args = message.get_args() # get payload from command /start payload = decode_payload(args) if payload: # we go here if we clicked on someone's link async with state.proxy() as data: data['user-id'] = payload # save the recipient ID in the context of the dialog await Stage.send_message_to.set() # set the state of sending the message to the recipient start_text = 'Hello! I am a bot for anonymous communication. Send a message to the person who posted this link. He won't know who sent the message.' await message.reply(start_text) return # if there is no payload, then the user just pressed /start link = await get_start_link(message.from_user.id, encode=True) # create a personal link content = f'Your personal link: {link}' await message.reply(content)

    让我们编写一个用于发送消息的处理程序：

     @dp.message_handler(state=Stage.send_message_to, content_types=ContentType.ANY) async def process_message(message: types.Message, state: FSMContext): inline_btn = InlineKeyboardButton('Reply', callback_data=f'answer_{message.from_user.id}') inline_kb = InlineKeyboardMarkup().add(inline_btn) async with state.proxy() as data: if 'answer_user_id' in data: user_id = data['answer_user_id'] # if we reply to a message await message.answer('Your response has been delivered.') await message.bot.send_message(user_id, 'You have received an answer.') else: user_id = data['user-id'] # if we send a new message await message.answer('Your message has been sent.') await message.bot.send_message(user_id, 'You have received a new anonymous message.') if message.video: await message.bot.send_video(user_id, message.video.file_id, reply_markup=inline_kb) content = message.video.as_json() elif message.video_note: await message.bot.send_video_note(user_id, message.video_note.file_id, reply_markup=inline_kb) content = message.video_note.as_json() elif message.voice: await message.bot.send_voice(user_id, message.voice.file_id, reply_markup=inline_kb) content = message.voice.as_json() elif message.photo: await message.bot.send_photo(user_id, message.photo[-1].file_id, reply_markup=inline_kb) content = message.photo[-1].as_json() elif message.audio: await message.bot.send_audio(user_id, message.audio.file_id, reply_markup=inline_kb) content = message.audio.as_json() elif message.sticker: await message.bot.send_sticker(user_id, message.sticker.file_id, reply_markup=inline_kb) content = message.sticker.file_id elif message.document: await message.bot.send_document(user_id, message.document.file_id, reply_markup=inline_kb) content = message.document.as_json() else: await message.bot.send_message(user_id, message.text, reply_markup=inline_kb) content = message.text await state.finish()

    在上面的示例中，我们实现了发送各种类型消息的功能，但您可以只保留文本。

    让我们为“回复”按钮编写一个点击处理程序：

     answer_regexp = re.compile("answer_(.*)") @dp.callback_query_handler(lambda c: answer_regexp.match(c.data), state=['*', Stage.send_message_to]) async def process_callback_answer(callback_query: types.CallbackQuery, state: FSMContext): await bot.answer_callback_query(callback_query.id) await bot.send_message(callback_query.from_user.id, 'Enter your reply.') async with state.proxy() as data: data['answer_user_id'] = answer_regexp.match(callback_query.data).group(1) await Stage.send_message_to.set()

    剩下的就是运行机器人：

     if __name__ == '__main__': executor.start_polling(dp, skip_updates=False)

    使用数据库

    让我们创建一个db.py文件并标记使用数据库的方法。

    连接到数据库：

     client = MongoClient('CONNECTION_STRING HERE') db = client['AskFM'] users = db['users'] messages = db['messages']

    添加用户：

     def add_user(user_id, username, datetime): user_filter = { 'user_id': user_id } user = users.find_one(user_filter) if user and user['status'] == 'deleted': update = { 'status': 'active' } users.update_one(user_filter, {'$set': update}) return elif user: return data = { 'user_id': user_id, 'username': username, 'datetime': datetime, 'status': 'active' } users.insert_one(data)

    删除用户：

     def delete_user(user_id): user_filter = { 'user_id': user_id } update = { 'status': 'deleted' } users.update_one(user_filter, {'$set': update})

    添加消息：

     def add_message(user_id_from, user_id_to, username_from, username_to, content, message_type, datetime): message = { "user_id_from": user_id_from, "user_id_to": user_id_to, "username_from": username_from, "username_to": username_to, "content": content, "message_type": message_type, "datetime": datetime } messages.insert_one(message)

    将数据库工作集成到机器人中

    让我们编写一个处理程序来跟踪机器人的激活和停用：

     @dp.my_chat_member_handler() async def add_to_channel(update: ChatMemberUpdated): if update.new_chat_member.status == 'member': db.add_user(update.from_user.id, update.from_user.username, time.time()) elif update.new_chat_member.status == 'kicked': db.delete_user(update.from_user.id)

    将用户注册添加到/start方法中：

     db.add_user(message.from_user.id, message.from_user.username, time.time())

    最终处理程序代码：

     @dp.message_handler(commands=['start'], state=['*', Stage.send_message_to]) async def start(message: types.Message, state: FSMContext): args = message.get_args() # get payload from /start command payload = decode_payload(args) if payload: # we go here if we clicked on someone's link async with state.proxy() as data: data['user-id'] = payload # save the recipient ID in the context of the dialog await Stage.send_message_to.set() # set the state of sending the message to the recipient start_text = 'Hello! I am a bot for anonymous communication. Send a message to the person who posted this link. He won't know who sent the message.' await message.reply(start_text) db.add_user(message.from_user.id, message.from_user.username, time.time()) # added here return # if there is no payload, then the user just pressed /start link = await get_start_link(message.from_user.id, encode=True) # create a personal link content = f'Your personal link: {link}' await message.reply(content) db.add_user(message.from_user.id, message.from_user.username, time.time()) # and added here

    如果我们有一个单独的处理程序来查看机器人的激活和停用，为什么要将用户注册添加到/start方法中？ 答案：该方法并不总是正确。

    将消息注册添加到消息发送处理程序的最末尾：

     user_to = await bot.get_chat_member(user_id, user_id) if 'answer_user_id' in data: message_type = 'answer' else: message_type = 'question' db.add_message(message.from_user.id, user_id, message.from_user.username, user_to.user.username, content, message_type, time.time())

    这是代码的结尾。 完成的代码可以在这里查看。

    我们启动机器人并尝试测试它：

    在下一部分中，我们将研究如何创建仪表板本身。

一旦被暂停，拉马诺夫将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到其暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，拉马诺夫将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，拉马诺夫的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己才能访问。

如果拉马诺夫没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从仪表板重新发布帖子。

笔记：

感谢您保证 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以执行以下操作来标记 Lamanoff：

使 lamanoff 的所有帖子不那么明显

举报其他不当行为

不标记拉马诺夫将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Maksim Lamanov

Posted on Jun 24

In this article, we will look at the importance of connecting basic analytics to a bot and what benefits it can bring.

Introduction

In today's digital world, where interaction with customers is increasingly happening through online platforms, creating and maintaining effective chatbots is becoming an important aspect of a successful business. However, building a bot that simply provides information or answers basic questions is no longer enough for a competitive advantage. Today, it is important to understand user behaviour and needs, analyse data and make informed decisions based on this information.

This is where connecting analytics to the bot comes to the rescue. The integration of analytical tools and methods allows you to collect, analyse and interpret data about user interaction with the bot. This gives us the opportunity to gain valuable insights that will help optimise the performance of the bot, improve the user experience and make informed decisions based on data.

Connecting analytics to a bot is an integral part of the development and improvement of chatbots in our dynamic digital age. It allows you to turn a bot from a simple communication tool into a powerful data analysis tool that will help your business form an effective strategy and achieve its goals. Let's look at all the advantages and opportunities that the connection of analytics to the bot opens up for us.

What data will we receive today

In this example, we will learn:

  • see the number of "live" and "dead" users;
  • see the number of messages sent through the bot;
  • see the messages themselves;
  • build charts.

Tools

  • Python 3 (aiogram - a framework for creating bots);

  • MongoDB - a database that will serve as a source for building a dashboard;

  • Telegram - is the best messenger for working with bots;

  • Redash - is a self-hosted service for building dashboards.

First, let's write a bot

As an example, let's write a simple Python 3 bot that allows you to exchange anonymous messages.

Principle of operation

The user is provided with a personal link to the bot, which contains the identifier of the user to whom the message needs to be sent. He can post it on his social networks. Anyone who wants to send an anonymous message to a user must follow this link. After that, the bot will receive the /start command with a payload in the form of a user ID in Telegram.

Create a bot in Telegram

Go to @BotFather and create a bot:

Connecting libraries

We connect the library for creating telegram bots:

And also for working with MongoDB:

Write the code

Create a main.py file and write the beginning for any bot:

bot = Bot(token='BotFather TOKEN HERE')
storage = MemoryStorage() # storage that allows you to remember the states of dialogs with users
dp = Dispatcher(bot, storage=storage)

Let's add one single state that we need to send messages:

class Stage(StatesGroup):
    send_message_to = State()

Let's write a handler for the /start command:

@dp.message_handler(commands=['start'], state=['*', Stage.send_message_to])
async def start(message: types.Message, state: FSMContext):
    args = message.get_args() # get payload from command /start
    payload = decode_payload(args)

    if payload: # we go here if we clicked on someone's link
        async with state.proxy() as data:
            data['user-id'] = payload # save the recipient ID in the context of the dialog

        await Stage.send_message_to.set() # set the state of sending the message to the recipient

        start_text = 'Hello! I am a bot for anonymous communication. Send a message to the person who posted this link. He won't know who sent the message.'
        await message.reply(start_text)
        return

    # if there is no payload, then the user just pressed /start
    link = await get_start_link(message.from_user.id, encode=True) # create a personal link
    content = f'Your personal link: {link}'
    await message.reply(content)

Let's write a handler for sending messages:

@dp.message_handler(state=Stage.send_message_to, content_types=ContentType.ANY)
async def process_message(message: types.Message, state: FSMContext):
    inline_btn = InlineKeyboardButton('Reply', callback_data=f'answer_{message.from_user.id}')
    inline_kb = InlineKeyboardMarkup().add(inline_btn)
    async with state.proxy() as data:
        if 'answer_user_id' in data:
            user_id = data['answer_user_id'] # if we reply to a message
            await message.answer('Your response has been delivered.')
            await message.bot.send_message(user_id, 'You have received an answer.')
        else:
            user_id = data['user-id'] # if we send a new message
            await message.answer('Your message has been sent.')
            await message.bot.send_message(user_id, 'You have received a new anonymous message.')
        if message.video:
            await message.bot.send_video(user_id, message.video.file_id, reply_markup=inline_kb)
            content = message.video.as_json()
        elif message.video_note:
            await message.bot.send_video_note(user_id, message.video_note.file_id, reply_markup=inline_kb)
            content = message.video_note.as_json()
        elif message.voice:
            await message.bot.send_voice(user_id, message.voice.file_id, reply_markup=inline_kb)
            content = message.voice.as_json()
        elif message.photo:
            await message.bot.send_photo(user_id, message.photo[-1].file_id, reply_markup=inline_kb)
            content = message.photo[-1].as_json()
        elif message.audio:
            await message.bot.send_audio(user_id, message.audio.file_id, reply_markup=inline_kb)
            content = message.audio.as_json()
        elif message.sticker:
            await message.bot.send_sticker(user_id, message.sticker.file_id, reply_markup=inline_kb)
            content = message.sticker.file_id
        elif message.document:
            await message.bot.send_document(user_id, message.document.file_id, reply_markup=inline_kb)
            content = message.document.as_json()
        else:
            await message.bot.send_message(user_id, message.text, reply_markup=inline_kb)
            content = message.text
    await state.finish()

In the example above, we implemented the ability to send various types of messages, but you can leave only text.

Let's write a click handler for the "Reply" button:

answer_regexp = re.compile("answer_(.*)")


@dp.callback_query_handler(lambda c: answer_regexp.match(c.data), state=['*', Stage.send_message_to])
async def process_callback_answer(callback_query: types.CallbackQuery, state: FSMContext):
    await bot.answer_callback_query(callback_query.id)
    await bot.send_message(callback_query.from_user.id, 'Enter your reply.')
    async with state.proxy() as data:
        data['answer_user_id'] = answer_regexp.match(callback_query.data).group(1)
    await Stage.send_message_to.set()

It remains to run the bot:

if __name__ == '__main__':
    executor.start_polling(dp, skip_updates=False)

Working with the database

Let's create a db.py file and mark up methods for working with the database.

Connecting to the database:

client = MongoClient('CONNECTION_STRING HERE')
db = client['AskFM']

users = db['users']
messages = db['messages']

Adding a user:

def add_user(user_id, username, datetime):
    user_filter = {
        'user_id': user_id
    }
    user = users.find_one(user_filter)
    if user and user['status'] == 'deleted':
        update = {
            'status': 'active'
        }
        users.update_one(user_filter, {'$set': update})
        return
    elif user:
        return
    data = {
        'user_id': user_id,
        'username': username,
        'datetime': datetime,
        'status': 'active'
    }
    users.insert_one(data)

Delete user:

def delete_user(user_id):
    user_filter = {
        'user_id': user_id
    }
    update = {
        'status': 'deleted'
    }
    users.update_one(user_filter, {'$set': update})

Adding a message:

def add_message(user_id_from, user_id_to, username_from, username_to, content, message_type, datetime):
    message = {
        "user_id_from": user_id_from,
        "user_id_to": user_id_to,
        "username_from": username_from,
        "username_to": username_to,
        "content": content,
        "message_type": message_type,
        "datetime": datetime
    }
    messages.insert_one(message)

Integrating work with the database into the bot

Let's write a handler that tracks the activation and deactivation of the bot:

@dp.my_chat_member_handler()
async def add_to_channel(update: ChatMemberUpdated):
    if update.new_chat_member.status == 'member':
        db.add_user(update.from_user.id, update.from_user.username, time.time())
    elif update.new_chat_member.status == 'kicked':
        db.delete_user(update.from_user.id)

Add user registration to the /start method:

db.add_user(message.from_user.id, message.from_user.username, time.time())

Final handler code:

@dp.message_handler(commands=['start'], state=['*', Stage.send_message_to])
async def start(message: types.Message, state: FSMContext):
    args = message.get_args() # get payload from /start command
    payload = decode_payload(args)

    if payload: # we go here if we clicked on someone's link
        async with state.proxy() as data:
            data['user-id'] = payload # save the recipient ID in the context of the dialog

        await Stage.send_message_to.set() # set the state of sending the message to the recipient

        start_text = 'Hello! I am a bot for anonymous communication. Send a message to the person who posted this link. He won't know who sent the message.'
        await message.reply(start_text)

        db.add_user(message.from_user.id, message.from_user.username, time.time()) # added here

        return

    # if there is no payload, then the user just pressed /start
    link = await get_start_link(message.from_user.id, encode=True) # create a personal link
    content = f'Your personal link: {link}'
    await message.reply(content)

    db.add_user(message.from_user.id, message.from_user.username, time.time()) # and added here

Why add user registration to the /start method if we have a separate handler that looks at the activation and deactivation of the bot? Answer: that method does not always work correctly.

Add message registration to the very end of the message send handler:

user_to = await bot.get_chat_member(user_id, user_id)
if 'answer_user_id' in data:
    message_type = 'answer'
else:
    message_type = 'question'
db.add_message(message.from_user.id,
               user_id, message.from_user.username,
               user_to.user.username, content,
               message_type, time.time())

This is the end of the code. The finished code can be viewed here.

We start the bot and try to test it:

In the next part, we will look at creating the dashboard itself.

Once suspended, lamanoff will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, lamanoff will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by lamanoff will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If lamanoff is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Note:

Thanks for keeping DEV Community safe. Here is what you can do to flag lamanoff:

Make all posts by lamanoff less visible

lamanoff consistently posts content that violates DEV Community's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging lamanoff will restore default visibility to their posts.

item.title: “Squoosh”是一款图像压缩应用程序 | "Squoosh" is an image compression application
item.description:
隆重推出 Squoosh，这是一款在线图像压缩应用程序，可在保持视觉质量的同时减小文件大小。 这是免费的。

操作环境

它适用于各种平台的浏览器。
这只是一个猜测，因为我找不到任何支持环境的描述，但预计它可以在以下平台上与 Chrome/Firefox/Safari 配合使用。

视窗苹果安卓
功能性和可操作性

它是一款非常高性能的应用程序，可以调整各种参数，同时比较压缩前后的图像质量/文件大小，并创建令人信服的图像。
同时，它还进行格式转换，例如，将相机拍摄的几MB的JPEG照片转换为高压缩比的WebP/AVIF格式，可以将尺寸大幅缩小到几百KB，同时保持表观图像质量。

下面是 252KB PNG 插图的屏幕截图，颜色数量减少并压缩至 52.1KB。 如果放大，您可以看到右侧压缩图像中的眼镜镜片和头发中出现抖动。

这里，故意夸张地减少了颜色，以便可以看到图像质量的差异，但通过适当的调整，可以将图像质量保持在即使眼睛疲劳也看不出来的程度。

其他功能包括图像在本地处理，而不发送到服务器。 这可以用于由于信息管理原因而难以使用在线工具的情况。

事实上，即使 Wifi 关闭，它也能工作。

但是，无法一次指定和压缩多个文件。
如果您想一次上传大量图像，这可能是一个缺点。

竞品比较

泰尼PNG

这是一个免费的在线应用程序，允许您将多个图像压缩在一起。 最多 20 张图像。

参数调整不可用。

我爱IMG

这是一个免费的在线应用程序，允许您将多个图像压缩在一起。 我不知道最大图像数量是多少，但我至少可以做 40 个。参数调整不可用。

除了压缩之外，您还可以调整大小和重新格式化。

免费版和付费版之间的功能差异

没有付费版本。
所有功能都是免费的。

官方网站

地点网址
家文档没有任何
教程没有任何

是那些我还没有真正确认可以工作的。 朱莉娅 👩🏻‍💻 - 2022 年 9 月 16 日

麦兹·斯托曼 - 2022 年 9 月 16 日

一旦被暂停，ayaco 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到其暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，ayaco 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，ayaco 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己才能访问。

如果 ayaco 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从仪表板重新发布帖子。

笔记：

感谢您保证 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以执行以下操作来标记 ayaco：

使 ayaco 的所有帖子不那么明显

举报其他不当行为

取消 ayaco 的标记将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Introducing Squoosh, an online image compression application that reduces file size while maintaining visual quality. It is Free.

Operating environment

It works with browsers of various platforms.
This is just a guess since I can't find any description of the supported environment, but it is expected to work with Chrome/Firefox/Safari on the following platforms.。

Windows Mac iPad iPhone Android
○ ○?1 ○ ○?1 ○

Functionality and operability

It is a very high-performance application that can tune various parameters while comparing the image quality/file size before and after compression, and create a convincing one.
At the same time, it also performs format conversion, so for example, by converting a JPEG photo of several MB taken with a camera into a WebP/AVIF format with a high compression ratio, it is possible to significantly reduce the size to several hundred KB while maintaining the apparent image quality.

Below is a screen shot of a 252KB PNG illustration with the number of colours reduced and compressed down to 52.1KB. If you zoom in, you can see that dither appears in the spectacle lenses and hair in the compressed image on the right.

Here, the colours have been deliberately reduced in an exaggerated manner so that the difference in image quality can be seen, but with appropriate tuning, the image quality can be maintained to such an extent that it cannot be seen even if you strain your eyes.

Other features include the fact that images are processed locally, without being sent to a server. This can be used in cases where online tools are difficult to employ for information management reasons.

In fact, it worked even when Wifi was switched off.

However, it is not possible to specify and compress multiple files at once.
In cases where you want to upload a large number of images at once, this may be a drawback.

Competitive comparison

TynyPNG

This is a free online application that allows you to compress multiple images together. Maximum of 20 images.

Parameter tuning is not available.

ILoveIMG

This is a free online application that allows you to compress multiple images together. I didn't know what the maximum number of images was, but I were able to do at least 40. Parameter tuning is not available.

Apart from compression, you can also resize and reformat.

Difference in functionality between free and paid versions

There is no paid version.
All features are free.

Official site

Site URL
Home squoosh.app/
Documentation None
Tutorial None

{.gridfc30p}

  1. "?" are those I have not actually confirmed to work. ↩

Julia 👩🏻‍💻 - Sep 16 '22

Mads Stoumann - Sep 16 '22

Once suspended, ayaco will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, ayaco will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by ayaco will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If ayaco is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Note:

Thanks for keeping DEV Community safe. Here is what you can do to flag ayaco:

Make all posts by ayaco less visible

ayaco consistently posts content that violates DEV Community's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging ayaco will restore default visibility to their posts.

item.title: 150 多个练习 JavaScript 基本概念的问题 | 150+ Questions to Practice JavaScript's Foundational Concepts
item.description:
它一次又一次地名列无数排行榜和许多开发者调查的榜首。

我真正喜欢 JavaScript 的一点是它非常容易学习。 入门非常简单，我什至不能再强调它。

您无需安装解释器/编译器或担心任何 IDE。

一旦你开始学习它，你甚至不必担心严格的打字，遵循任何样板（如 Java），或者让自己经历单调的构建/编译阶段。

实际上，所需要的只是一个浏览器和一个简单的文本编辑器，尽管如今没有人会使用通用编辑器（例如记事本），因为我们拥有出色的编辑器，可以提供类似 IDE 的体验，例如Visual Studio Code 。

无论如何，回到主题，鉴于 JavaScript 如此出名，越来越多的人正在学习它或想要学习它也就不足为奇了。

在这方面，有大量与 JavaScript 相关的问题需要回答，以确保我们在学习这些概念后能够真正了解它们，这确实是富有成效的。

不久前，我在CodeGuage上发布了一个名为Questions的新功能（名字很简单，不是吗？ ）。 顾名思义，它的目的是提供一系列与CodeGuage 上给定课程相关的问题。

学习者可以快速浏览问题，以确保他们精通基础主题。

对于 JavaScript，我目前收集了150 多个问题（准确地说是 156 个），重点关注 CodeGuage 的JavaScript 课程的基础单元。

在这篇文章中，我的目标是列出我目前在 CodeGuage for JavaScript 上遇到的所有问题，以便能够吸引更广泛的受众，然后感兴趣的人可以在 CodeGuage 上查看问题的答案。

希望这些问题能够给大家带来新的学习。 让我们开始。

所有答案都可以在JavaScript 基础问题 - CodeGuage中找到。

问题

所有问题均根据其适用的章节进行分组。 如果你读完本章，你就会得到所有问题的答案。

介绍

  1. 谁创建了 JavaScript？
  2. 当我们说 JavaScript 是一种脚本语言时，意味着什么？
  3. 当我们说 JavaScript 是一种高级语言时，意味着什么？
  4. 说出 JavaScript 的一种实现。
  5. 我们在哪里编写 JavaScript 代码才能在浏览器中运行？
  6. 什么是外部 JavaScript 文件？
  7. 列出为网页使用外部 JavaScript 文件的 3 个好处。

安慰

  1. 控制台是什么？
  2. 当我们说控制台是执行 JavaScript 的 REPL 环境时，是什么意思？
  3. 控制台的唯一功能是执行 JavaScript。 对或错？ 如果是假的，那它还能做什么呢？
  4. 如何将任何内容记录到控制台？

基本

  1. 什么是整数？
  2. 什么是花车？
  3. 对或错？ 如果错误，请给出正确的解释。
  4. 说出 JavaScript 提供的任意五个基本算术运算符及其符号。
  5. 当我们说 JavaScript 中乘法运算符的优先级高于加法运算符时，这是什么意思？
  6. 我们如何强制一个特定的操作先于另一个操作发生？
  7. 什么是字符串？
  8. 给出在 JavaScript 中表示字符串的三种方法。
  9. 什么是转义序列？
  10. 列出任意三个转义序列。
  11. 如何在单引号或双引号字符串中表示新行？
  12. 什么是字符串连接？
  13. 什么是变量？
  14. 如何在 JavaScript 中创建变量？
  15. 在 JavaScript 中定义术语“关键字”。 举一个关键字的例子。
  16. 什么是变量声明？
  17. 什么是变量初始化？
  18. 什么是变量赋值？
  19. 变量初始化与变量赋值有何不同？
  20. 在 JavaScript 中，变量可以保存任意类型的值。 对或错？ 如果错误，请给出正确的解释。
  21. 说出可以使用 JavaScript 显示的三种不同类型的对话框。
  22. 给出可用于显示这些对话框的相应函数。
  23. 即使单击“取消”按钮， prompt()也始终返回一个字符串。 对或错？ 如果错误，请给出正确的解释。
  24. 如何将字符串转换为数字？

变量

  1. 使用var声明变量有哪些不同的变体？
  2. 说出 JavaScript 中命名变量的三个规则。
  3. 如何检查给定变量是否存在？
  4. 什么是变量提升？
  5. 给出let和var之间的两个区别。
  6. 使用let创建的变量的临时死区是什么意思？

常数

  1. 编程中的常量是什么？
  2. 如何在 JavaScript 中定义常量？
  3. 可以在没有初始值设定项的情况下定义常量。 对或错？ 如果错误，请给出正确的描述。
  4. 什么是尖叫蛇外壳？为什么在编程中常用它来命名常量？
  5. 评论
    1. 编程中的注释是什么？
    2. 给出注释在编程中的两种用途。
    3. 说出 JavaScript 中两种注释的名称。
    4. 如何写各种评论？
    5. 给出写好评论的两个技巧。

    数据类型

    1. 什么是数据类型？

举报其他不当行为
